title: Full Workflow Challenge
subtitle: Putting it all together

sections.content: You've learned every part of the git workflow individually. Now it's time to put it all together: initialize a repository, make commits, create branches, merge them, and push to a remote — the complete developer workflow.
sections.analogy: This is like your first morning doing the entire routine without checking the instructions. You know each step — making coffee, packing lunch, catching the bus. Now you'll do them all in sequence, start to finish, from memory.
keyPoints: Real developers run through this cycle many times a day
Confidence comes from repetition — this challenge builds muscle memory

sections.instruction:
Build a portfolio site using the full git workflow. Initialize a repo, make commits, create a branch, merge it, and push to GitHub.

Target workflow:
1. git init
2. Add content to index.html, stage, and commit
3. Create a branch for styles
4. Add styles to style.css, stage, and commit
5. Switch back to main and merge
6. Add a remote and push
sections.prompt: Complete the full git workflow. Start with `git init` and end with `git push`.
sections.hint: Start by initializing the repository: git init
hints:
Add content: echo "

Portfolio

" > index.html, then git add . and git commit -m "Initial commit"
Add styles: echo "body { color: navy; }" > style.css, then git add . and git commit -m "Add styles"
Merge: git checkout main, then git merge add-styles
Push: git remote add origin https://github.com/user/portfolio.git, then git push -u origin main

onSuccess.message: You just completed the full git workflow! From init to push — you initialized a repo, made commits, branched, merged, and pushed to a remote. This is exactly what professional developers do every day.

completionMessage: Level 4 complete! You've mastered git — the tool that every developer uses every single day.
